channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Philippine Government Requests Meta to Strengthen Measures Against Fake News on Social Media
item.description: The Philippine government has formally requested Meta, the parent company of Facebook, to implement stricter measures against the spread of fake news on its platforms. In a joint letter dated April 10...

item.title: DA holds first day of elective conference amid leadership transition and structural reforms
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has started the first day of its elective conference at Gallagher Estate in Midrand, signalling a potential shake-up in the party’s leadership and structure. Solly Msimang...

item.title: Vogue Williams Enjoys Family Time in St. Barth's, Showcasing Fitness
item.description: Model and presenter Vogue Williams was recently spotted enjoying a family vacation in St. Barth's, where she showcased her impressive physique while paddleboarding with her children. Images of William...

item.title: World Bank President Warns of Reduced Global Growth and Higher Inflation from Middle East Conflict
item.description: World Bank President Ajay Banga has warned that the ongoing war in the Middle East will seriously affect the global economy. Even if the fragile ceasefire announced by US President Donald Trump holds,...

item.title: Pakistan’s Diplomatic Outreach During US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Since the outbreak of the US-Iran conflict on February 28, 2026, Pakistan’s top civilian leadership has been actively engaged in telephonic diplomacy. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif initiated calls to ...

item.title: Study Finds Common Vitamin and Mineral Deficiencies Among People With Type 2 Diabetes
item.description: A large global review of 132 studies involving more than 52,000 adults with type 2 diabetes found that many people with the condition are deficient in key micronutrients, particularly vitamins D and B...

item.title: Waiian Shares His Journey of Authenticity and Growth in Filipino Hip-Hop
item.description: Makati-based rapper Waiian, known for his heartfelt and vulnerable lyrics, has steadily risen in the Filipino hip-hop scene. He discovered his love for lyricism in senior high school after analyzing L...

item.title: PHC grants temporary relief to Afghan family facing deportation amid pending UNHCR resettlement case
item.description: The Peshawar High Court has temporarily stopped law enforcement agencies from deporting an Afghan family whose asylum and resettlement case through the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (U...

item.title: Nine arrested for attempting illegal harvesting in Robben Island Marine Protected Area
item.description: Nine individuals have been arrested for attempting to illegally harvest marine resources in the Robben Island Marine Protected Area (MPA), according to Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environm...

item.title: US and Iranian Delegations Meet in Islamabad Amid Tense Peace Talks
item.description: Iranian and US delegations have arrived in Islamabad to commence high-stakes peace talks amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The Iranian team is led by parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghal...

item.title: PM Modi Highlights West Bengal Rally Attendance Amid High Temperatures
item.description: On Saturday, Prime Minister Narendra Modi shared his experience from a major public rally in Purba Bardhaman, West Bengal, emphasising the enthusiasm of attendees despite the scorching heat. In a post...

item.title: Ray Dalio warns of escalating global conflicts and historical patterns pointing to potential world war
item.description: Billionaire investor Ray Dalio has sounded a stark warning that the world is edging closer to a large-scale conflict, comparable to historical world wars. Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates and ...

item.title: Foreign Hunters Cancel Trips to Gilgit-Baltistan Amid Regional Unrest, Causing Rs250m Loss
item.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East has seriously affected the annual trophy hunting programme in Gilgit-Baltistan, leading to heavy financial losses for both local communities and the government. Acco...

item.title: Nigeria’s FTSE Frontier Market Upgrade Boosts Stock Market by N1.36 Trillion in Three Days
item.description: The Nigerian stock market sharply rose within just three days after FTSE Russell upgraded Nigeria from 'Unclassified' to Frontier Market status. This upgrade triggered significant buying from both loc...

item.title: Dayoo Shares How Personal Challenges Inspired His Motivational Song
item.description: Bongo Flava artist Dayoo, whose real name is Baltazary Eligy Romani, recently opened up about the personal experiences that inspired his hit song 'Siku ya Mwisho'. The song emerged from a health scare...

item.title: Quezon City Government Alerts Seniors on Scam Using Fake Birthday Cake Deliveries to Rob Homes
item.description: The Quezon City government has issued a strong warning to senior citizens and their families to remain vigilant against criminals who use a 'birthday cake' modus to gain entry into homes and commit ro...

item.title: Family in Thika Searches for 12-Year-Old Girl Missing After Going to Shop
item.description: A worrying incident in Thika has left a family in distress after their 12-year-old daughter, Nelius Muthoni, disappeared on December 24, 2025. The young girl was sent by her mother to a local shop in ...

item.title: ANC confirms stance on alliance as SACP opts for independent election participation
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) has clarified its position following a Special National Executive Committee (NEC) meeting, called to address the evolving dynamics within the revolutionary Alliance...

item.title: Sport as a Tool for Peace, Unity and Community Development in Tanzania
item.description: In Tanzania, sport and peace go hand in hand, creating strong foundations for unity, social cohesion and development. Sport provides a shared platform where people from different backgrounds, cultures...

item.title: Artemis II astronauts return safely to Earth after 10-day lunar mission
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully concluded as the Orion capsule, named Integrity, splashed down off the coast of southern California after a historic 10-day journey around the moon. The crew, co...
